mechanism_narrative: Increases in FEDFUNDS over the prior 3 monthly observations raise equity discount rates and reduce the present value of S&P 500 cash flows, producing negative forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if rolling 3-month increases in FEDFUNDS are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: A downward crossing in RSAFS 3-month growth below zero signals weakening household demand and lower expected corporate revenues, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS 3-month growth crossings below 0% do not produce lower subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered months at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises while PAYEMS growth slows, labor income expectations deteriorate and recession risk reprices upward, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months and PAYEMS 3-month growth decelerates versus the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint UNRATE rise and PAYEMS deceleration trigger has nonnegative average 60-day S&P 500 forward returns in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Positive INDPRO momentum over the prior 3 monthly observations signals improving real activity and operating leverage, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO momentum has a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 for 60-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation momentum is positive while FEDFUNDS is already high, equity investors price a longer restrictive policy path, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL 3-month percent change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction trigger is followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A HOUST rebound above its 6-month moving average signals improving housing demand and construction-linked earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST crossings above the 6-month moving average are not associated with higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than baseline months at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A UNRATE crossing above 4.5% signals rising slack and deteriorating earnings visibility, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE crossings above 4.5% have positive median 20-day S&P 500 forward returns in walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS growth is positive while FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%, strong demand keeps policy restrictive and delays rate relief, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 5% and RSAFS 3-month percent change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if this joint high-rate and positive-RSAFS-growth trigger has average 5-day S&P 500 forward returns greater than or equal to zero out of sample.
